verse.name: v1
verse.lines: T'was a day in early spring time, by an ancient wayside well, Eliezer paused to rest his camel train; He had found a bride for Isaac Ere the evening shadows fell, And his weary journey had not been in vain.

verse.name: v2
verse.lines: So he took the fair Rebekah, decked in jewels rich and rare Back to Abraham and Sarah far away. Where Rebekah loved her Isaac, And he loved Rebekah fair; Oh, it must have been a happy wedding day.

verse.name: v3
verse.lines: Oh get ready, the evening shadows fall, Don't you hear the Eliezer call? There's going to be a wedding supper, our joys will soon begin, In the evening when the camel train comes in.

verse.name: v4
verse.lines: The blessed Holy Spirit, through the prophet of this day, Has come down to earth to claim a worthy Bride. For our Isaac over yonder, Has prepared His tents of Love, And He wants His fair Rebekah by His side.

verse.name: v5
verse.lines: We have left our kinfolk gladly, We've bid the world goodbye; We're going to a Land beyond the sea. We shall soon behold our Isaac In the blest Eternity; What a happy, happy meeting that will be.
